title: Disabling server-side encryption of S3 buckets is security-sensitive
description: Disabling server-side encryption (SSE) for Amazon S3 buckets exposes stored data to potential unauthorized access. SSE ensures that data at rest is encrypted, protecting it from compromise if the storage media is accessed by unauthorized parties. Disabling SSE increases the risk of data breaches and may violate compliance requirements.
message: S3 bucket without server-side encryption detected. Enable SSE to protect data at rest.
impact: Unencrypted S3 buckets can lead to data breaches, regulatory violations, and loss of customer trust if sensitive data is exposed.
recommendation: Always enable server-side encryption for S3 buckets. Use 'aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration' in Terraform to enforce encryption by default.

noncompliant.description: S3 bucket is created without server-side encryption.

compliant.description: S3 bucket is created with server-side encryption enabled.

devsecops.security: Ensures data at rest is protected from unauthorized access.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD pipelines should enforce encryption policies for all S3 buckets.
devsecops.compliance: Meets regulatory requirements for data protection (e.g., PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR).

why_is_this_an_issue: Without server-side encryption, data stored in S3 buckets is vulnerable to unauthorized access and may not meet compliance requirements.
how_to_fix: Update S3 bucket configurations to include a server_side_encryption_configuration block. Test to ensure all buckets are encrypted by default.

security_mappings.CWE: CWE-311: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
security_mappings.CERT: MSC61-J: Do not use insecure or weak cryptographic algorithms
